ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (5776 Vacation Blvd. With 5770 Vacation Blvd, Somerset Ca)
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Ms. Boessenecker stated that the customary commissions rate in El Dorado County is 6% as indicated by the Guardian and the Examiner (El Dorado County) in the declaration she e-filed yesterday. The sale was confirmed at $83,500.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed February 24, 2017. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order and the declaration). Bond is not required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for the San Francisco Public Administrator, Kat Kozic present.

The Court asked counsel if she is confident that all potential heirs have been found. Ms. Boessenecker stated that the difficulty in amending the petition is what they are working with, that they conducted an extensive search, the connections are step family who seemed to have had the closest relationship with the decedent, that on the maternal side they were able to go up through the grandparents, that regarding the paternal side the have Martha Meinster, are trying to investigate if William McDonald had other children and if so they would knock out Ms. Meinster but no one has come forward. Ms. Boessenecker responded to an issue regarding the petition stating that schedule 8 is who the heirs are and that they have identified relatives but that does not make them heirs. The Court stated that it includes all persons named or checked in all other sections and that it is not just a list of heirs. Ms. Boessenecker stated that in her 23 years of practice she has never listed all of the relatives, that to extend duty to name all of the relatives goes beyond what is required and confirmed that everyone was given notice. The Court stated that maybe in giving notice the purpose of that section is satisfied. Ms. Boessenecker stated that they listed the issue of the predeceased spouse but do not know who the blood heirs are, that to check the box that Martha Meinster is the sole heir is premature, that the issue that remains is if William McDonald has other issue and that they have real property that needs to be dealt with. The Court took the Petition for Letters of Administration under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
